an7.34:1.1: “Last night, monks, a certain devatā in the far extreme of the night, her extreme radiance lighting up the entirety of Jeta’s Grove, came to me and, on arrival, bowed down to me and stood to one side. As she was standing there, she said to me,
an7.34:1.2: ‘These seven qualities, lord, lead to a monk’s non-decline.
an7.34:1.4: Respect for the teacher, respect for the Dhamma, respect for the Saṅgha, respect for training, respect for concentration, compliance, having admirable friends.
an7.34:1.5: These seven qualities, lord, lead to a monk’s non-decline.’
an7.34:1.7: Having said it, she bowed down to me, circled me three times, and then disappeared right there.”
an7.34:2.1: Respecting the Teacher, respecting the Dhamma,
an7.34:2.2: and with fierce respect for the Saṅgha,
an7.34:2.4: and with fierce respect for training,
an7.34:3.4: one is right in the presence of unbinding.
